examples.name: Coding task prompt assembly
examples.description: A feature-implementation task with system identity, repository rules, file context, tools, and verification requirements.

instruction_layers.title: System identity
instruction_layers.content: You are an expert software engineer. You write clean, testable, well-typed code.

instruction_layers.title: Repository conventions
instruction_layers.content: This project uses Python with Pydantic. All models must be validated. Use ruff for linting. Tests run with pytest.

instruction_layers.title: Task goal
instruction_layers.content: Add a RetryPolicy model to the execution environment schema. It should support max_retries, backoff_factor, and retryable_errors.

context_blocks.title: models/execution_environment.py
context_blocks.content: Current file with FilesystemPolicy, NetworkPolicy, ResourceLimits, etc. Add RetryPolicy alongside them.

context_blocks.title: tests/test_execution_environment.py
context_blocks.content: Test file for execution environment. Follow the pattern of existing test classes.

tool_exposures.description: Read a file from the filesystem
Edit a file with exact string replacement
tool_exposures.input_schema_summary: file_path: string, old_string: string, new_string: string

tool_exposures.description: Run pytest on a specific file or directory

constraints.title: Scope constraint
rules: Do not modify files outside models/ and tests/

constraints.title: Safety rules
rules: Do not modify existing public API signatures

messages.content: You are an expert software engineer building a harness for AI coding agents. Follow the instruction layers, respect constraints, and verify before finishing.

Add a RetryPolicy model with max_retries, backoff_factor, and retryable_errors fields. Add validation: max_retries must be >= 0, backoff_factor must be > 0, retryable_errors must be non-empty. Update tests and examples.

examples.name: Debugging task with failure context
examples.description: A debugging task that includes failure context blocks and repository instructions.

instruction_layers.title: System identity
instruction_layers.content: You are a senior debugging engineer. Find root causes, do not apply superficial fixes.

instruction_layers.title: Debug task
instruction_layers.content: Investigate and fix the test failure in test_durable_state.py. The error suggests a validation issue with ResumePointer.

context_blocks.title: models/durable_state.py
context_blocks.content: Durable state schema. ResumePointer has can_resume and resume_reason fields with a model_validator.

context_blocks.title: Test failure: test_cannot_resume_without_reason
context_blocks.content: FAILED test_durable_state.py::TestResumePointer::test_cannot_resume_without_reason - AssertionError: assert 'resume_reason is required' in '...ValidationError...'

context_blocks.title: tests/test_durable_state.py
context_blocks.content: Test file with ResumePointer test cases. The failing test expects a specific error message.

context_blocks.title: Previous run summary
context_blocks.content: The test was added in P7. The exact error message from the model_validator may not match what the test expects.

tool_exposures.input_schema_summary: file_path: string, old_string: string, new_string: string

constraints.title: Debug discipline
rules: Read the error message first before making changes

verification.summary: Confirm the fix resolves the failure
done_definition: Root cause is documented in the fix

messages.content: You are a debugging specialist. Investigate systematically, reproduce before fixing, verify after.
The test test_cannot_resume_without_reason is failing. Find the root cause and fix it.

examples.name: Report-generation task with artifact verification
examples.description: A documentation task verified by acceptance checks and artifact presence.

instruction_layers.title: System identity
instruction_layers.content: You are a technical writer. Produce clear, structured documentation with examples.

instruction_layers.title: Project conventions
instruction_layers.content: Documentation is written in Markdown. Code examples must be runnable. Diagrams use Mermaid.

instruction_layers.title: Report goal
instruction_layers.content: Generate a usage guide for the verification schema. Include: check types, evidence types, verdict logic, and an end-to-end example.

context_blocks.title: models/verification.py
context_blocks.content: The verification schema source code with all models, enums, and validators.

context_blocks.title: tests/test_verification_schema.py
context_blocks.content: Test file with example usage patterns for the verification schema.

context_blocks.title: Existing documentation style
context_blocks.content: Previous primitives were documented with a README-style guide. Focus on practical usage, include code snippets, and end with a full example.

tool_exposures.input_schema_summary: file_path: string, old_string: string, new_string: string

constraints.title: Content standards
rules: All code examples must use Python 3.11+ syntax
Include at least one full end-to-end example
Do not copy the entire source file into the guide

done_definition: Guide includes a runnable end-to-end example

messages.content: You are a technical writer for an AI agent harness project. Produce clear documentation with practical examples.
Write a usage guide for the verification schema. The audience is developers integrating verification into their agent workflows.
